title: `ERROR: Assert sig_macc.count(n->y) == 0 failed` occurs when multiple `$add` cells drive the same signal.

features.name: multiple_add_cells_same_output
features.description: Code should include two $add cells that drive the same output signal, created from multiple always @(*) blocks each containing an addition operation.

features.name: conflicting_add_drivers
features.description: Code should include a register output driven by two different always @(*) blocks, each performing addition with shared input signals.

features.name: macc_merge_conflict
features.description: Code should include multiple $add cells targeting the same Y output wire, which triggers alumacc pass assertion when attempting to create $macc.
features.snippet:
cell $add \c0
  connect \Y \o
end
cell $add \c1
  connect \Y \o
end
